item.title: There Is Nothing Inevitable About a Russian Victory in Ukraine
item.description: If Russia has all the cards, why has it achieved so little?

item.title: We Are Finally Free From Khamenei’s Suffocating Gaze
item.description: Now that Ayatollah Khamenei is dead, will another face replace his and carry on a version of the same story?

item.title: The Iran War Is Trump’s War
item.description: Has the president’s unerring instinct for vulnerability finally failed him?

item.title: JD Vance Has His Reasons
item.description: The vice president’s political transformation from Trump skeptic to MAGA superstar has many roots.

item.title: Tyra Banks’s Good Intentions for ‘America’s Next Top Model’ Went Awry
item.description: “America’s Next Top Model” was bad. The reality of fashion was often worse.

item.title: The Great Lie of War
item.description: Ben Rhodes, a contributing Times Opinion writer, discusses why Trump waged war on Iran — and why his predecessors didn’t dare to.

item.title: The Coming Iranian Revolution
item.description: Ayatollah Khomeini’s bait-and-switch game in the early years of the Islamic regime turned many of the people against it. They’ve been fighting the revolution since.

item.title: Trump Was Never Antiwar
item.description: Trump’s foreign policy has often been less a repudiation of neoconservatism than a mutation of it.

item.title: The Reason Gen Z Isn’t Dating
item.description: Clavicular is a symptom of a larger disease.

item.title: Trump, Iran and a Host of Questions
item.description: Questions about the future of the attack on Iran, and other reader perspectives. Also: Drug ads as a resource for patients.

item.title: Don’t Let Trump Ruin the World Cup
item.description: The World Cup is a chance for the U.S. to show it is more than Trump.

item.title: Nine Law Firms Surrendered. Four Law Firms Won.
item.description: The four law firms that fought Trump’s executive orders have forced him to retreat.

item.title: Trump’s Foreign Policy Vision: Imperialism
item.description: Donald Trump’s foreign policy vision is imperialism

item.title: War and Peace Cannot Be Left to One Man — Especially Not This Man
item.description: The Constitution isn’t a technicality.

item.title: Gavin Newsom’s Ego May Be the Democrats’s Biggest Advantage
item.description: California’s governor challenges the usual criteria for a presidential nominee.

item.title: The End of the Illusion for Cuba’s Regime
item.description: Without a political transformation, Cuba’s economic recovery will remain an illusion.

item.title: Hospice Care Is a Mess
item.description: Too many hospice care responsibilities are placed on families.

item.title: Americans Need to Know More About The Iran Attacks
item.description: The American public has received too little information to effectively judge the goals and objectives of the largest U.S. military operation in the Middle East in a generation.

item.title: A Tyrant Falls. Dangerous Uncertainty Begins.
item.description: No one should mourn Ayatollah Khamenei, but Trump is creating the opportunity for chaos and violence with his campaign against Iran.

item.title: After the Attack on Iran, Fear and Support
item.description: Readers debate the justification for President Trump’s decision to take military action against Iran.

item.title: Trump May Come to Regret This
item.description: The old U.S.-led order is dead. The new one feels unstable and ominous, as if a storm could descend at any moment.

item.title: How to Think About Trump’s War With Iran
item.description: Ending the Islamic republic’s regime will be long and arduous, but perhaps a softer version will emerge.

item.title: The Whooping Crane Recovery Shows What We Stand to Lose
item.description: The whooping cranes’ fragile recovery highlights the need for renewed commitment to safeguarding America’s endangered species.

item.title: Iran Got Trump All Wrong
item.description: We should not squander this moment, when Iran is uniquely weak and vulnerable and we hold all of the advantages — literally.

item.title: Trump’s New Tariffs Invite Corruption
item.description: The president still has a lot of leeway to impose tariffs. That’s an invitation for businesses to curry favor with him.

item.title: Friedrich Merz Has Bigger Problems Than Trump
item.description: Friedrich Merz’s chancellorship is in choppy waters.

item.title: Paramount Bought Warner Bros. Discovery, But Nobody’s Happy
item.description: Anxiety about Netflix gives way to anxiety about layoffs.

item.title: With Iran, Trump and Netanyahu are Doing the Free World a Favor
item.description: American hearts should be moved by moments like this, when free nations band together to administer justice and supply hope.

item.title: Trump’s Attack on Iran Is Reckless
item.description: Donald Trump promised voters that he would end wars, not start them.

item.title: This Senator Is a Relic of a Gentler G.O.P. How Long Can He Hold On?
item.description: John Cornyn says his challenger doesn’t have the character to serve in the Senate. MAGA voters might not care.

item.title: The Outlook Is Grim for a Freer Iran
item.description: U.S. military interventions have often gone poorly for the countries in question. But there is much the world, and Iranians, can do.

item.title: The Attack on Iran: A Range of Voices
item.description: Readers offer varied perspectives, including praise and criticism, on the strikes again Iran.

item.title: Music, at Least, Doesn’t Lie
item.description: In an age in which facts are losing their meaning, the performing arts can ground us in what is true.

item.title: The Man Who Destroyed Iran
item.description: The supreme leader organized his existence around one big idea: resistance.

item.title: Regime Change in Iran? 4 Opinion Writers on Khamenei Strike.
item.description: How should Americans feel about the potential for regime change? What happens now?

item.title: Guess What Trump Values Above the Constitution
item.description: The State of the Union was very revealing.

item.title: A War of Choice Does Not Make Us Safer
item.description: “The first mission of a president is to keep Americans safe,” says Times columnist Nicholas Kristof following the United States and Israel’s military strike against Iran Saturday. “In this case I fear that President Trump, by embarking on a war of choice, has made us less safe and may be putting Americans at risk for months or years to come.”

item.title: War With Iran Is a Mistake
item.description: We don’t know how likely it is that the attacks will be successful, nor what costs or risks they bring.

item.title: The President of War
item.description: The U.S. military was once a tool of last resort for American presidents.

item.title: How Israel Lost Americans
item.description: Netanyahu and his government deserve the growing bipartisan opprobrium they’re receiving.

item.title: The Pentagon Can’t Afford This A.I. Fight
item.description: The military needs the very best A.I. to streamline its operations. It should find ways to work with these companies, not erect barriers.

item.title: Real Despots Hijack Artificial Intelligence
item.description: Don’t give Trump and Hegseth unfettered control of dangerous A.I.

item.title: Will Trump’s Failures Cost the G.O.P. in Texas?
item.description: The round table convenes to discuss the upcoming Texas primaries and Trump’s State of the Union shortcomings.

item.title: If A.I. Is a Weapon, Who Should Control It?
item.description: Better to put this power in the hands of private industry or government officials?

item.title: Will Trump’s Failures Cost the G.O.P. in Texas?
item.description: The round table convenes to discuss the upcoming Texas primaries and Trump’s State of the Union shortcomings.

item.title: Trump’s Sins Are His Own. Leave Pagans Out of It.
item.description: Readers respond to a guest essay on the president’s behavior and the ancient roots of human dignity and compassion.

item.title: The Epstein Files and Our Broken Public Trust
item.description: Readers discuss an Opinion guest essay by Daniel Richman. Also: A response from Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche; President Trump and seniors’ health.
